thesis: Bloom Energy is a hypergrowth solid-oxide fuel cell manufacturer with a $20B backlog and clear demand tailwinds from AI data center electrification. Q1 2026 revenue surged 130% YoY to $751M with raised FY2026 guidance of $3.4–3.8B. Non-GAAP operating income expanded 10x to $130M and adjusted EBITDA grew 6x to $143M. The 2.8 GW Oracle deal and $5B Brookfield partnership validate commercial demand at scale. High valuation (~11x forward sales) and execution risk warrant a speculative buy rating with disciplined position sizing.

drivers: Data center electrification boom drives backlog expansion beyond $20B
Gross margin expansion to 36%+ as manufacturing scales
Hydrogen transition opens zero-carbon TAM and justifies premium multiples
International expansion into Europe and Asia

risks: Guidance miss or margin compression from supply chain bottlenecks
Customer concentration — loss of Oracle or Brookfield delays
Multiple compression if growth decelerates from 80% to 40%
Competition from PEM fuel cells or battery storage achieving cost parity

base_case.assumptions: FY2026 guidance met at midpoint ($3.6B revenue, 34% gross margin), backlog converts steadily, no major customer losses, and margin expansion continues into 2027.

catalysts.event: Q2 2026 Earnings — test of guidance trajectory and margin sustainability
Q3 2026 Earnings — backlog conversion and FY2027 guidance preview
FY2027 Guidance — will reveal if 80% growth is sustainable

key_risks: Customer concentration on mega-deals (Oracle 2.8 GW, Brookfield $5B)
Execution risk scaling manufacturing to $3.8B revenue run-rate
Valuation risk at ~11x forward sales — any deceleration triggers severe compression
Technology competition from PEM fuel cells and battery storage
Natural gas price volatility affecting customer economics
